disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No exertional trigger, no radiation to left arm/jaw, no diaphoresis, no breathlessness; symptom tied to meal and recumbency and partly antacid-relieved
trigger_question_examples: Is it brought on or worsened by physical exertion, and does it spread to your arm or jaw?
Any sweating or breathlessness with it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — it's not exercise-related, doesn't go to my arm or jaw, no sweating and my breathing's fine; it came on with the meal and lying down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Typical reflux features — burning rising to neck, sour regurgitation, hypersalivation, worse recumbent/post-prandial, chronic night cough/morning hoarseness
trigger_question_examples: Is there a sour acid taste/regurgitation, and is it worse lying down after meals?
Any long-standing night cough or morning hoarseness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, sour acid comes up, it's worse lying down after eating, and I've had a night cough and morning hoarseness for a while.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No haematemesis/coffee-ground vomit, no melaena, no dysphagia/odynophagia, no weight loss (GI alarm screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any vomiting of blood or coffee-ground material, or black tarry stools?
Any difficulty or pain swallowing, or weight loss?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood or coffee-ground vomit, no black stools, no trouble swallowing and no weight loss.
